terminologyProfile.descriptionRule: Use measurable 3D graphics terms. Avoid vague words unless they are paired with concrete geometry/material/shader parameters.

formLanguage: broad axial palace composition with large ceremonial negative spaces
layered Korean timber halls with dark hip-and-gable tiled roofs
low urban belt and dense green tree belt behind the palace
asymmetric mountain skyline with a lower rocky left ridge and a taller right peak
aerial perspective with warm foreground courts and cool hazed distance
structureKind: deep action-ready hierarchy of palace systems and repeated architectural bays
staged foreground, palace, vegetation, city, and mountain depth layers
deterministic instanced vegetation and urban massing
custom deformed roof and ridge terrain geometry
motionPotential: hinged outer and inner gate leaves
topology-preserving Layer Lens visibility and emphasis states
materialFamilies: charcoal ceramic roof tile with layered ridge and eave response
painted timber and restrained dancheong accents
pale stone and plaster podiums and walls
warm sandy courtyard with compacted path variation
cool receding city masonry and glazing
objectClass.notes: The target is the complete photographed palace campus and its city-tree-mountain context, not an exact survey model of a named building. The 842x476 source supports an approximate stylized real-time reconstruction. Existing preview geometry is provenance only and must not be promoted into the production base.

reasoning: The source is a crowded architectural landscape rather than an isolated object, so identity depends on the relationship between many systems rather than one silhouette.

The foreground outer gate, broad processional courts, inner gate, main hall, secondary halls, walls, corridors, tree belt, city, and mountains are all separately readable and require a deep hierarchy.
Korean roof identity depends on curved lifted eaves, layered fascia, pitch, overhang, ridge caps, end ornaments, rafters, and tile rhythm; plain prisms cannot satisfy the reference.
The mountain skyline is composition-critical and asymmetric. It requires custom seeded ridge geometry, layered forest and rock response, and atmospheric recession rather than cone primitives.

The low-resolution single aerial view hides rear faces, exact bay counts, and fine dancheong patterns. Those details must be inferred conservatively and documented instead of claimed as recovered geometry.
Production variants, camera sockets, gates, Layer Lens groups, colliders, and protected architecture require stable action-ready nodes even though most architecture remains static.

specDepthDecision.rationale: A shallow palace-block plus cone-mountain scene fails the reference. Production readiness requires six staged macro systems, separate halls/gates/courts/corridors, reusable roof topology, deterministic city/vegetation/terrain repetition, independent material layers, and multi-view visual evidence.

resolvedInferenceLimits: Rear and hidden side elevations are not visible; infer them bilaterally from the visible axial and side-wing logic.
Exact historic building identity, dimensions, bay counts, and roof ornament profiles are below source resolution; use reference-consistent proportions rather than exact reconstruction claims.
The source contains baked midday lighting, haze, and camera response; material channels cannot be uniquely recovered and must pass confidence-gated extraction plus neutral relighting.
Distant city block identity is unreadable; preserve bounded massing, road gaps, and atmospheric value structure rather than individual buildings.

definitionOfDone: At the reference aerial camera, the scene immediately reads as the photographed Seoul palace campus rather than a generic East Asian compound.
The outer gate, inner gate, and main throne hall form a clear receding axis across broad warm courtyards with controlled entrances and side walls.
Every primary hall uses a reusable Korean hip-and-gable roof system with curved lifted eaves, layered fascia, ridge and end caps, tile or rafter rhythm, and credible podium-column-roof contact.
The palace remains the identity focus while the city, tree belt, and mountain layers create the source depth ordering without random clutter.
The mountain silhouette preserves the lower rocky left ridge, central saddle, and taller steep right peak under reference framing.
The asset exposes deterministic seeds, stable layer groups, named cameras and sockets, gate pivots, collider metadata, protected destruction groups, and production-safe variant controls.
The final base and every curated production variant have fresh full-image comparison evidence with an overall AI-vision score of at least 0.86 and every selected critical feature at least 0.86.

featureGroups.name: Outer gate to inner gate to main hall axial hierarchy
qualityCriteria: The outer gate anchors the lower-left foreground, the inner gate crosses the central processional axis, and the main hall remains the largest palace focal mass.
Each gate and hall has a stable pivot, podium contact, controlled opening, and parent link inside the palace-axis system.
The hierarchy remains unchanged across all production variants.
failureModes: the gates and main hall read as equal toy blocks
the processional axis bends or loses the ordered gate count
secondary halls compete with or obscure the main hall

featureGroups.name: Korean hip-and-gable tiled roof system
qualityCriteria: Primary roofs show pitched hip-and-gable planes, center sag, lifted corners, layered eaves, fascia, ridge caps, hip ridges, and end ornaments.
Tile rows and rafters follow the deformed roof surface with deterministic spacing and safe instancing.
Columns and beams visibly support roof loads through podium contact; no roof floats above a generic box.
failureModes: plain triangular prism or flat slab roofs
uniform eaves without lifted corners or layered fascia
tile, ridge, or ornament repetition detaches from the roof surface

featureGroups.name: Courtyard, wall, and ceremonial negative-space proportions
qualityCriteria: The foreground and main processional courts remain broad enough to dominate the palace ground plane.
Perimeter walls and corridor wings frame rather than fill the courts, with entrances aligned to the palace axis.
Tree and city distributions preserve the large source clearings.
failureModes: courtyards shrink into narrow paths between oversized halls
walls or vegetation close the ceremonial sightline
random detail removes the reference negative spaces

featureGroups.name: City to tree belt to mountain atmospheric depth
qualityCriteria: Low bounded city massing sits behind the palace with explicit road and green gaps.
A darker tree belt separates urban blocks from mountain foothills.
Successive mountain layers reduce contrast, saturation, and detail with haze while keeping the palace foreground readable.
failureModes: city blocks become random high-rise clutter
tree belt and mountains collapse into one green wall
haze crushes palace roof contrast or erases the skyline

featureGroups.name: Asymmetric mountain silhouette and aerial composition
qualityCriteria: The left skyline stays lower, broad, and rock-patched; the central saddle remains open; the right peak is steeper and highest.
Mountain terrain is custom seeded ridge geometry rather than cones or symmetric noise mounds.
The reference camera preserves the palace-to-sky balance and the mountain peaks remain inside the 1.769 frame.
failureModes: cone mountains or a mirrored skyline
the right peak is lower than the left ridge
camera framing crops the skyline or gives mountains more visual weight than the palace

visualDeltaChecks: outer-gate, inner-gate, and main-hall centerline and projected-size delta
courtyard width-to-depth, wall-height, and occupied-to-empty ground-plane delta
roof pitch, overhang, eave lift, ridge length, and tile/rafter rhythm delta
city/tree/mountain depth ordering, value range, and haze delta
left-ridge, central-saddle, and right-peak skyline delta
roof, timber, stone, courtyard, vegetation, mountain, and city albedo/roughness/normal/AO response delta
reference camera field of view, elevation, target, horizon, and palace-to-sky balance delta
Layer Lens isolation delta without topology, parent-link, socket, or collider mutation
antiShallowSpecRules: Do not proceed to code if qualityContract.qualityBar is unassessed.
Do not proceed to code if the spec only contains a root component for a moderate or complex object.
Do not proceed to code if required featureGroups are not represented by componentTree, materials, or repetitionSystems.
Do not proceed to code if visible local features are described only in prose and not attached to components/materials/evidenceRefs.
Do not proceed past structural-pass if attached child parts lack attachment.parentSocket, localStart, localEnd, embedDepth/overlap, and gapTolerance.
Do not pass material look-dev when albedo is reused as roughness, height, normal, or AO.
Do not pass material look-dev without macro, meso, and micro surface frequency bands for close-up materials.
Do not pass reference-fidelity material look-dev from a source image without usable referencePbr maps or an explicit documented limitation.
Do not patch a spec with extracted PBR maps when extraction confidence is below the target threshold unless the user explicitly accepts lower fidelity.
Do not reuse, upscale, or cosmetically polish the preview cone-mountain or toy-block implementation as the production base.
Do not use cone primitives for mountains or triangular prisms as the final Korean roof system.

Do not continue a visual pass without a browser-rendered screenshot, one full reference/render comparison, AI-vision layer and semantic-feature scores, concrete mismatch notes, and an evidence-bound review entry.
Do not let a global score hide a selected critical feature below 0.86.

Do not change the palace axis, gate count or order, main-hall identity, roof topology, parent links, sockets, action pivots, reference camera, collider topology, or protected review targets in production variants.
Do not generate production variants until the production base is meaningful and accepted through the locked pass gates.
Do not claim exact palace dimensions, exact hidden elevations, or physically recovered PBR from this single low-resolution image.

mustMatch: outer gate to inner gate to main hall axial hierarchy
curved Korean hip-and-gable tiled roof rhythm
broad ceremonial courtyard and wall proportions
city to tree belt to mountain atmospheric depth
asymmetric lower-left to higher-right mountain silhouette
niceToHave: side corridor and pavilion bay detail
restrained column, podium, and dancheong accents

visualAcceptance.scoringRule: AI vision must inspect a side-by-side reference/render sheet and score the current pass from 0 to 1. Pixel-diff code may assist diagnostics but cannot approve a pass.

featureReviewPolicy.selectionRule: Choose only the most visually salient, identity-defining, user-prioritized, or high-risk semantic systems. Group repeated parts instead of reviewing every mesh. AI vision scores every selected feature from the same full reference/render pair.

specRefineTriggers: local feature not traceable to viewEvidence
codeRefineTriggers: spec is adequate but generated geometry/material does not match
lighting hides geometry or material response
stopCriteria: target fidelity reached or user accepts current approximation
remaining gaps require new reference images or manual art

screenshotPolicy.minimumEvidence: Each visual pass needs a reference image, rendered screenshot, side-by-side comparison sheet, AI vision score, layer scores, and critique before choosing continue.
screenshotPolicy.reviewPairRule: Compare the same camera/viewpoint whenever possible; do not judge a front reference against a random render angle.
screenshotPolicy.acceptanceAuthority: AI vision review of the comparison sheet. Code-generated pixel similarity is not sufficient evidence.

featureReviewTargets.name: Outer gate, inner gate, and main hall hierarchy
Curved hip-and-gable roof, ridge, eave, tile, and rafter system
Courtyard, walls, and processional negative space
City, tree belt, and mountain atmospheric layers
Lower rocky left ridge, central saddle, and taller right peak
Side corridor and pavilion framing system
Podium, column, beam, and dancheong accents
Foreground modern strip and wall contact

referencePbrExtraction.acceptedLimitation: The low-resolution source cannot support mandatory extraction for every material. Regional roof, courtyard, and mountain attempts still run; confidence below 0.7 is recorded as a stop signal and procedural evidence is used without recovered-PBR claims.

regionalResults.usage: Regional palette and roughness evidence only; not claimed as exact recovered PBR.
Regional palette and roughness evidence only; not claimed as exact recovered PBR.
Atmospheric value evidence only because the low-resolution mountain crop contains sky and haze; procedural forest/rock maps remain authoritative.

mustAvoid: plastic-looking smooth bark, stone, cloth, foliage, or aged material
local color/detail described only in prose without material masks
claiming exact PBR recovery when confidence is below the target threshold
reference lighting copied without separating material readability

screenshotReview: Compare albedo palette and local color zones.
Compare cavity dirt, edge wear, stains, moss, scratches, or other local masks.
Compare key/fill/rim structure, exposure, tone mapping, background, and contact shadows.
Capture a neutral-light render to verify material readability without reference lighting.
Capture a grazing-light close-up to expose flat normals, uniform roughness, tiling, and plastic highlights.
Capture a reference-matched render from the same camera framing as the source.

actionReadiness.contract: Every declared component resolves through an exact semantic runtime alias to a live Object3D, while every unique socket and collider is SHA-bound to its captured parent and local transform.
requiredComponentFields: attachment for child appendages, connectors, limbs, tubes, handles, legs, horns, wings, branches, or cables
authoringRules: Do not collapse independently movable parts into one mesh.
Put transforms on component pivot groups, not only on raw meshes.
For attached child parts, put the pivot at the semantic root/socket and build visible geometry from localStart to localEnd.
Represent hinge, socket, detachable, and breakable intent even when no animation is implemented yet.
Use simplified collider proxies for runtime physics instead of visual mesh colliders by default.

destructionPolicy.fractureGroupNaming: Use stable semantic names such as body-shell, left-hinge, glass-panel, branch-segment.
destructionPolicy.debrisStrategy: Prefer detachable component groups and a small number of procedural fragments over random mesh explosion.

assumptions: The scene is an approximate stylized reconstruction from one low-resolution aerial view, not photogrammetry or an exact reverse-engineered palace model.
Hidden rear and side elevations use bilateral inference from visible axial halls, corridor wings, and Korean timber-building conventions.
Exact bay counts, historic ornament profiles, and individual distant city buildings are below source resolution and remain intentionally approximate.
The palace axis, gate order, main-hall identity, reference camera, sockets, pivots, and collider topology are protected production invariants.

coordinateFrame.front: -Z faces the foreground reference camera; the ceremonial axis recedes toward +Z
coordinateFrame.scaleReference: 1 world unit equals approximately 10 meters; palace campus spans about 70 by 120 units

silhouette.boundingShape: wide 1.769 aerial landscape with low palace masses in the lower half and asymmetric mountain ridges across the upper middle
aspectRatios: palace occupied width is approximately 0.86 of frame width
main processional court depth is approximately 0.32 of visible campus depth
right mountain peak is approximately 1.35 times the projected height of the left ridge
silhouette.symmetry: palace axis is approximately bilateral while side compounds, city massing, vegetation, and mountain skyline are intentionally asymmetric

observations: Palace axis occupies the lower center with broad sandy courts and dark roofs.
City, tree belt, and mountain layers recede behind the palace.
Mountain skyline is lower and broader on the left and highest on the right.
Long perimeter wall crosses the lower frame.
Outer gate sits left of center with three openings.
Modern dark-roof strip occupies the lower right edge.
Large warm courts dominate the campus.
Inner gate and main hall align on a receding axis.
Side walls and corridors preserve open ceremonial space.
Dark roofs have deep overhangs, lifted corners, layered ridges, and repeated tile rhythm.
Main hall roof is the largest and most elevated.
Pale podiums and dark columns support the roof masses.
Long low corridors and side compounds frame the central axis.
Pavilions vary in size but remain subordinate to the main hall.
Low varied urban blocks form bounded bands.
Road and green gaps break the city mass.
Contrast is lower than palace foreground.
Dense green tree belt separates palace and city from mountain foothills.
Broad palace clearings remain free of random trees.
Left ridge is broad and lower with light granite patches.
Center drops into a saddle with distant blue ridges.
Right peak is steep and tallest with forest-dominant cover.

componentTree.name: Seoul Palace Hero Root

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: non-rendered stable root pivot containing six independently addressable macro systems

details: Preserve base topology independently from production variants.

componentTree.name: Foreground perimeter system

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: long foreground strip, perimeter wall, outer gate, and first ceremonial clearing

surfaceDetail.notes: Keep foreground subordinate to the palace axis.

details: Layer Lens Palace includes the perimeter and outer gate.

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: ordered court-gate-hall hierarchy on a protected centerline
geometryDescriptor.normalStrategy: generated vertex normals plus independent normal fields

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: low repeated corridor bays and subordinate pavilion clusters framing the center

surfaceDetail.notes: Do not crowd the processional courts.

componentTree.name: Palace and foothill vegetation system

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: semantic instanced clusters with exclusion masks preserving palace clearings

details: Instance trunks and canopy clusters separately.

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: three bounded low-rise bands with explicit road and green gaps
geometryDescriptor.normalStrategy: flat and weighted normals by block class

surfaceDetail.notes: City must not compete with the palace.

componentTree.name: Asymmetric mountain and atmosphere system

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: seeded triangulated ridge strips and height fields with explicit left, saddle, right, and distant profiles

details: Nature lens includes mountain terrain and atmosphere.

geometryDescriptor.topologyIntent: low dark-roof linear buildings and road edge
segmented plaster wall with tiled cap and gate openings
three-opening gate with podium, columns, beams, and curved Korean roof
broad low ground plane preserving negative space
largest protected sandy clearing between inner gate and main hall
wide gate with central opening, columns, beams, and curved roof
largest two-tier podium hall with deep column grid and protected curved hip-and-gable roof topology
secondary axial hall with single podium and curved roof
small repeated secondary halls preserving axial depth
segmented court walls with controlled axial openings
long low repeated column and roof bays
long low repeated column and roof bays
bounded subordinate pavilions with varied footprints
bounded subordinate pavilions with varied footprints
small side clearings bounded by pavilions
dark dense canopy band with irregular top edge
bounded clusters outside ceremonial exclusion masks
density-scaled forest instances following mountain slopes
dense low-rise blocks with limited height classes
lower-density blocks preserving the mountain saddle
sparse low blocks merging into tree belt
explicit negative-space strips through city bands
broad authored ridge strip with shallow summit and granite masks
lower valley profile exposing distant blue ridge layers
steep authored ridge and height field with highest skyline point
two low contrast ridge strips visible through the central saddle

materials.name: Charcoal ceramic roof tile

albedo.samplingNotes: Preserve dark blue-charcoal roofs without crushing them to black.

colorVariation.pattern: tile-row mottling and weathered ridge caps

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: One tile-row period remains stable across hall scales.

surfaceFrequencyBands.role: ceramic grain and grazing highlight breakup

roughness.localResponse: slightly smoother ridge caps and rougher drainage cavities

ambientOcclusion.notes: Tile overlaps, fascia, and ridge intersections only.

shaderNotes: Independent albedo, roughness, height/normal, and AO fields.
Curved eaves, ridges, and tile-row silhouette remain geometry.
materials.notes: Attempt regional reference extraction before material-pass; low confidence remains a documented stop signal.

materials.name: Painted structural timber

albedo.samplingNotes: Muted red-brown columns and beams, subordinate to roof mass.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: Stable grain width across columns and beams.

roughness.localResponse: smoother hand-height edges and rougher beam cavities

ambientOcclusion.notes: Beam-column joints and recessed door leaves.

shaderNotes: Independent deterministic PBR fields; no shared channel textures.

materials.name: Restrained dancheong accent

albedo.samplingNotes: Small cool-green, ochre, and vermilion beam bands only.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: Band width remains consistent across hall sizes.

shaderNotes: Keep accents below five percent of frame area.
materials.notes: Inferred conservatively because source resolution does not reveal exact patterns.

materials.name: Pale stone and plaster

albedo.samplingNotes: Podiums, walls, and thresholds remain pale but not white.

roughness.localResponse: darker rough base and slightly smoother thresholds

ambientOcclusion.notes: Podium steps, wall caps, and column contacts.

shaderNotes: Independent mineral albedo, roughness, normal, and AO.
materials.notes: Contact shadow must not turn pale podiums black.

materials.name: Warm ceremonial courtyard

albedo.samplingNotes: Warm sandy courts with broad subtle compaction zones.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: No visible tiling from the aerial camera.

roughness.localResponse: slightly smoother compacted axis and rougher margins

ambientOcclusion.notes: Marker bases, walls, and podium contacts only.

shaderNotes: Broad macro variation must not become noisy speckle.
materials.notes: Attempt regional reference extraction before material-pass.

materials.name: Palace vegetation

albedo.samplingNotes: Tree belt is darker than palace clusters.

roughness.localResponse: slightly lower roughness on sun-facing canopy tops

shaderNotes: Use instanced trunk and canopy clusters with deterministic vertex color.

materials.name: Mountain forest

albedo.samplingNotes: Near ridges are dark green; distant layers shift cooler.

roughness.localResponse: ravines remain rough and dark without black crush

shaderNotes: Atmospheric recession is lighting/fog plus bounded material shift.
materials.notes: Attempt regional reference extraction before material-pass.

materials.name: Exposed mountain granite

albedo.samplingNotes: Light irregular granite patches are most visible on the left ridge.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: Stable rock grain across terrain scale.

roughness.localResponse: slightly smoother exposed faces and rougher forest contacts

shaderNotes: Silhouette-affecting facets use terrain geometry; micro grain uses normal.

materials.name: Distant urban masonry

albedo.samplingNotes: Cool receding city palette with low contrast.

textureProjection.texelDensityIntent: Facade rhythm remains subpixel from reference camera.

shaderNotes: No high-frequency random windows that compete with palace roofs.

materials.name: Distant urban glazing

albedo.samplingNotes: Muted cool glazing, not transparent at aerial scale.

shaderNotes: Keep opacity at one to avoid sorted transparency costs.

materials.name: Distant atmospheric layer

albedo.samplingNotes: Cool blue-green distant ridges and haze.

shaderNotes: Use restrained linear or exponential fog; no post-process double haze.
materials.notes: ACES tone mapping is applied once at renderer output.

sculptDNA.authoringRule: Only finish and bounded density controls may vary. Palace axis, gate count and order, main-hall identity and roof topology, parent links, sockets, action pivots, reference camera, collider topology, and protected review targets remain hard invariants.

repetitionSystems.name: Curved roof tile rows
repetitionSystems.distribution: surface-following rows over deformed roof planes

repetitionSystems.name: Painted eave rafters
repetitionSystems.distribution: even spacing under front and rear eaves

repetitionSystems.name: Ridge and end ornaments
repetitionSystems.distribution: ridge endpoints and selected hip ridges

repetitionSystems.name: Tree trunks and canopy clusters
repetitionSystems.distribution: semantic density masks outside palace clearings

repetitionSystems.name: Bounded city blocks
repetitionSystems.distribution: three district bands with explicit road and green gaps

repetitionSystems.name: Mountain granite patches
repetitionSystems.distribution: slope and elevation masks weighted toward the left ridge

repetitionSystems.name: Mountain forest breakup
repetitionSystems.distribution: terrain-following density field with ravine and rock exclusions

acceptance: Silhouette reads correctly without materials.
Quality contract has named all required macro feature groups before code generation.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Build the component hierarchy implied by the pre-spec complexity assessment.
acceptance: Macro, meso, and repeated structures meet qualityContract.minimumSpecDepth.
Parent-child relations, joints, seams, sockets, and contact points are explicit.
Every attached child appendage/connector has parentSocket, localStart/localEnd, contactType, embedDepth or overlap, and gapTolerance.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Refine shape, deformation, bevels, tapers, curves, asymmetry, and visible local geometry.
acceptance: Important visible forms are represented in component geometryDescriptor, deformations, localFeatures, or repetitionSystems.
Endpoint-based child parts are rooted at their attachment sockets and do not visibly float away from parents.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Match material color, roughness, bump, and local variation.
acceptance: Reference-derived albedo palette records dominant, secondary, and accent colors per visible material.
Each important material defines roughness variation and at least one normal/bump/displacement response.
Local material overrides, dirt/wear/stains/moss/chips/scratches or equivalent masks are tied to evidenceRefs.
Thin, transparent, reflective, wet, or fibrous materials document alpha/transmission/clearcoat/metalness/fiber response when relevant.
Generated preview uses procedural albedo/roughness/bump texture or vertex color variation instead of one flat color.
Generated preview uses independent PBR maps at 1024px or higher for the quality-first tier.
If source pixels are available, referencePbr extraction passed at confidence >= 0.7 or the pass is stopped/requesting better references.
Macro, meso, and micro surface frequency bands are visible at the intended review distance without obvious tiling.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Add procedural surface locality such as normal/bump/displacement, AO, dirt, stains, chips, grain, moss, scratches, and wear.
acceptance: Every required material feature group has local overrides or surfaceDetail tied to evidenceRefs.
A grazing-angle close-up proves that normal/height detail breaks highlights naturally and does not read as smooth plastic.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Make material and form readable under neutral turntable lighting plus optional reference lighting.
acceptance: lightingFromPhoto identifies key light direction/color/intensity, fill light, rim or environment light, and ambient color.
Exposure, tone mapping, background color/gradient, shadow softness, and contact shadow behavior are specified.
Lighting does not hide geometry/material gaps and screenshots can be compared fairly to the reference.
Neutral, grazing, and reference-matched lighting checks distinguish material errors from lighting errors.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Make the model ready for future animation, transformation, physics, or destruction.
acceptance: Macro and movable meso components have stable pivot nodes.
Sockets, collider proxies, and destruction metadata are present for future runtime actions.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Protect runtime performance after visual fidelity is accepted.
acceptance: Triangle count, draw calls, instancing, LOD strategy, and FPS target are documented or verified.
Repeated detail is instanced or simplified where possible without breaking silhouette/material believability.

featureReviews.notes: The exact SHA-bound corrected-seed production pair confirms palace-axial-hierarchy remains visible for blockout.

visualEvidence.notes: The accepted corrected-seed seoul-palace-hero-v002 full-image pair is reused to verify the inherited blockout contract.
visualEvidence.aiVisionNotes: The palace hierarchy and courts remain intact. Denser right-side vegetation slightly reduces spatial clarity without obscuring the principal architecture. Independent pixel-level AI-vision review found no broken geometry, roof tearing, seed-specific collapse, or loss of gate/hall hierarchy. The variants remain topology-consistent and environmentally distinct. Simplified city blocks, spherical vegetation, faceted terrain boundaries, and the single source view limit fidelity claims.

lodPlan.strategy: full component tree and material layers
merge static components and reduce local feature geometry

performanceBudget.optimizationPolicy: Use instancing and spatial groups after accepted visual fidelity. Do not remove reference-critical roof, courtyard, axis, or skyline geometry merely to reduce polygons.

lightingFromPhoto: Key light: warm-neutral directional sun from upper-left/front at approximately 5600 K, intensity near 2.6, one 2048 directional shadow map with restrained soft contact edges.
Fill light: cool sky hemisphere from above and behind, intensity near 1.2, preserving pale walls and dark roof midtones.
Rim/environment light: broad cool back-right environment cue, intensity near 0.55, separating mountain ridges and roof silhouettes without a second shadow map.
ACES filmic tone mapping is applied exactly once with exposure near 1.05; background uses a clear blue gradient and restrained linear depth haze.
Ground and podium contact shadows remain localized; ambient occlusion supports column, wall, tree, and city contacts without globally darkening the scene.
proceduralStrategy: Block out the reference aerial composition with authored palace, city, tree-belt, and asymmetric mountain masses before any surface detail.
Build every gate, court, hall, corridor, city band, vegetation group, and ridge layer under stable semantic pivots with serialization-safe runtime maps.
Use a reusable Korean roof factory based on deformed extruded roof planes, lifted eave curves, fascia, ridge and hip caps, end ornaments, rafters, and instanced tile rows.
Generate mountains from authored seeded ridge profiles and triangulated terrain strips; never use cones.
Generate bounded city districts and vegetation from semantic masks that preserve road, green, and ceremonial negative spaces.
Generate independent deterministic 1024px albedo, roughness, height/normal, and AO fields with macro, meso, and micro frequency bands.
Attempt regional reference PBR extraction for roof, courtyard, and mountain pixels without low-confidence override; record sub-0.7 results as stop signals.
Apply one ACES output transform, one directional shadow map, restrained key/fill/rim intent, contact shadows, and atmospheric depth haze.
Complete and visually approve each locked pass before implementing the next pass or initializing production variants.
animationAnchors: ReferenceCamera, AxisStart, OuterGate, InnerGate, MainCourt, MainHall, RearHall, MountainLookout, and VariantAnchor sockets
outer and inner gate leaf hinge pivots with bounded opening angles
reference-aerial, palace-axis, side-depth, mountain-silhouette, roof-grazing, and hierarchy-interaction camera anchors
Layer Lens Full, Palace, City, and Nature visibility/material-state controls
destructionAnchors: Architecture, courts, walls, terrain, city, and vegetation are protected static groups.
Only named lightweight ridge ornaments and banners may detach at explicit sockets.
No whole-building fracture or random explosion behavior is permitted.
risks: Single low-resolution aerial reference limits exact bay counts, hidden elevations, ornament profiles, and material recovery.
The wide scene may exceed draw-call or memory budgets if repeated roof, city, and vegetation systems are not instanced.
Dark roof tiles can crush to black under aggressive contact shadow or tone mapping.
Random city or vegetation density can erase ceremonial clearings and palace identity.
Symmetric procedural terrain can destroy the reference mountain composition.
Reference-derived PBR extraction may fail the 0.7 confidence threshold because pixels contain baked lighting and mixed materials.
